channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts condemns elected officials for intimidating judges
item.description: Chief Justice John Roberts condemned elected officials who have intimidated judges and defied court rulings, highlighting them in his year-end report Tuesday as part of what Roberts called “illegitimate activity” that threatens the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden doesn't believe he should have dropped out of race: Source
item.description: President Joe Biden told aides privately that he believes he could have defeated Donald Trump had he remained in race
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: How immigration and border security dominated 2024 and decided an election
item.description: Immigration and border security were two issues that dominated 2024 and help decide the November election as the border crisis loomed large over voters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Treasury says Chinese hackers remotely accessed workstations, documents in 'major' cyber incident
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chinese hackers remotely accessed several U.S. Treasury Department workstations and unclassified documents after compromising a third-party software service provider, the agency said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A guide to funeral services for former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Funeral services honoring former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday at age 100, will be held in Georgia and Washington, D.C., beginning Saturday, Jan. 4, and concluding Thursday, Jan. 9. Here's a list of events, as provided by the Joint Task Force-National Capital Region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Learning Curve: The new players in Congress
item.description: Every two years, the period between November's election and when the new Congress begins is often the busiest swath of time for the media's coverage of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sen. Blumenthal applauds law that expands Social Security benefits
item.description: WASHINGTON (WTNH) -- Nearly 3 million Americans could see a boost in Social Security payments, thanks to a bipartisan act awaiting President Joe Biden's signature.
item.dc:creator: Eva Zymaris, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about H-1B visas at the center of Musk-MAGA fight
item.description: A simmering debate over work visas exploded over the last days of the year, exposing a rift over immigration among President-elect Trump’s closest supporters.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China hacked Treasury Department earlier this month: Officials
item.description: The bad actor overrode security measures and remotely accessed workstations and "certain unclassified documents" from users.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter is being mourned in his tiny hometown and around the world
item.description: Tributes to former President Jimmy Carter are pouring in from his small hometown of Plains, Georgia, and around the world.
item.dc:creator: Russ Bynum, Sharon Johnson And Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden admin suppressed intel officials' views that supported COVID-19 lab leak theory: report
item.description: Advocates of the COVID lab-leak theory within the federal government were blocked from sharing their opinions and research with the broader intel community, according to sources inside the FBI and other government officials familiar with the Biden administration's internal efforts during the ea
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border arrests are little changed in December, ending Biden's term at lowest level
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Arrests for illegally crossing the border from Mexico in December are little changed from a month earlier, a U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ford Museum will open display honoring Jimmy Carter's life
item.description: After the death of former President Jimmy Carter, the Gerald R. Ford Museum in Grand Rapids is opening a new display to commemorate his life.
item.dc:creator: Anna Skog, NewsNation

item.title: What we know about Jimmy Carter funeral plans
item.description: Former President Carter's funeral will be held in Washington early next month as the culmination of roughly a week of remembrances, President Biden announced in a proclamation late Sunday. Carter's state funeral will take place at Washington National Cathedral on Jan. 9, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A caucus of military veterans seeks to bridge the political divide in a polarized Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The players in this year's Army-Navy football game kept up a long tradition, with each side honoring the other’s school song when the contest ended. It was an acknowledgment that the future could bring moments when the opponents that day are teammates on deadlier fields.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter is an example of ‘simple decency': Joe Biden
item.description: President Biden said former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday, “lived a life measured not by words but by deeds.
item.dc:creator: Brian Howard, NewsNation

item.title: Geraldo Rivera recalls Jimmy Carter's life, legacy
item.description: "He was a wonderful president and even better in his post-presidential life," Geraldo Rivera said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Fight over spending stopgap foreshadows funding challenges in new Congress
item.description: The messy government shutdown fight this month foreshadows some of the challenges House Republicans could face next year, as a major test on funding awaits the incoming GOP “trifecta.” Congress narrowly averted a government shutdown last week — but not without a bit of drama.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘contemptible fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC
